name: SlingWithCheck
description:
Library component. Models the PROPOSED fix for #3768: `gt sling <formula> <agent>` with a precondition close-step. Real-source intent: between sling.go:912 (lock acquire) and sling.go:918 (hookBeadWithRetry), insert a `bd.Close(siblings, formula)` call that closes any same-formula Hooked beads on the same assignee. The new bead row then becomes the only Hooked bead for that (assignee, formula) tuple.

v0.2: split the lock-granted transition by sibling state -- only send Close if sibling is Hooked, otherwise skip directly to Hooking. This matches real bd.Close semantics (it operates on the existing Hooked-set, not on Open beads) AND fixes a model-level race: a stale Close to an Open bead could be delivered after that bead became Hooked by another Sling, transitioning it Hooked->Closed and stranding the other Sling in HookSent.

Flow:
  Idle
  -> AcquiringLock     (receive INVOKE + send Acquire)
  -> ClosingSibling    (receive Granted + send Close, if sibling=Hooked)
  OR -> Hooking        (receive Granted, if sibling!=Hooked)
  -> Hooking           (from ClosingSibling, when sibling=Closed; close has landed)
  -> HookSent          (send Sling to HOOK_OUT)
  -> Releasing         (when target=Hooked; mirrors synchronous hookBeadWithRetry)
  -> Done              (send Release)

This component is paired with BeadCloseable (lib/beadcloseable.kinner.json) -- the Bead variant with a CLOSE in-port that closes a Hooked bead.

INVOKE.description: patrol or external trigger invokes the sling subcommand. Tagged 'Sling'.

LOCK_ACQUIRE_OUT.description: send Acquire to the per-assignee lock. Tagged 'Acquire'.

LOCK_GRANTED_IN.description: receive Granted from the per-assignee lock once it's free. Tagged 'Granted'.

LOCK_RELEASE_OUT.description: release the lock after the new bead row has been hooked. Tagged 'Release'.

CLOSE_OUT.description: send Close to the same-formula sibling Bead, but only if the sibling is currently Hooked. Tagged 'Close'.

SIBLING_OBSERVE.description: observe the same-formula sibling Bead's lifecycle state. Determines whether to send Close (if Hooked) and gates the post-close transition (waits for sibling=Closed).

TARGET_OBSERVE.description: observe the target Bead's lifecycle state. Sling waits in HookSent until target is observed Hooked before releasing the lock -- mirrors real-world synchronous hookBeadWithRetry.

HOOK_OUT.description: fires the hook event at the target bead. Tagged 'Sling'.

actors.name: SlingWithCheck
actors.owner: gt sling subcommand WITH proposed fix. Branches at AcquiringLock based on sibling state: send Close only if sibling Hooked, else skip to Hooking directly.

triples.event: lock granted, sibling is Hooked: send Close
lock granted, no sibling Hooked: proceed directly to Hooking
sibling close delivered (sibling Closed): proceed to Hooking
target now Hooked: hook is committed, safe to release lock
release lock (sling.go: implicit on function return)
